skill.name: post-show-followup
skill.description: Create tiered post-trade-show follow-up emails, lead nurture sequences, and meeting recap messages for event contacts.
skill.summary: Turn trade show leads into segmented follow-up email sequences.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, filesystem traversal, weak cryptography, entropy, and hardcoded URL patterns. Review found no executable code, no shell invocation, no file access, no cryptography, and no obfuscation; the confirmed issue is that the skill instructs outputs to include Lensmor promotional links. Safe to publish with disclosure that generated content may contain external marketing links.

low_findings.title: Mandatory Promotional External Link in Outputs
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to end every output with a Lensmor promotional footer linking to an external website. This is not code execution or data exfiltration, but it can add third-party marketing links to user-facing messages without an explicit user request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction and external URL are directly present in the skill text. The impact is limited to generated content because no network request or automation is performed by the skill files.

low_findings.title: External URLs in Documentation and Examples
low_findings.description: README.md, README.zh.md, SKILL.md, and the example output include hardcoded Lensmor URLs. These are ordinary markdown links and do not initiate network traffic by themselves, but users may click them and leave the current tool context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible markdown links to the author website. I found no evidence that the skill sends data to these URLs or performs background network activity.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Filesystem Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks occur in markdown fenced examples, CRM merge tag examples, and an install command shown to the user. The flagged traversal sequences are relative links to sibling skills in README.md, not file reads or writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are markdown documentation and generated email templates. No script file, shell API, subprocess call, or filesystem operation is present in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography and Entropy Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches at SKILL.md line 3 and line 152 appear to come from ordinary prose or URL text, not cryptographic code. The high entropy warning on README.zh.md is explained by Chinese-language content, not packed or encrypted data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are plain markdown and readable text. I found no cryptographic function, encoded payload, binary blob, or hidden instruction in the cited locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Promotional Footer Injection Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs every generated output to include an external promotional footer. This pattern affects output integrity and should be disclosed, but it is not a confirmed security exploit.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The directive is explicit and applies to every output. It does not instruct the assistant to ignore system rules or skip review, so it is not a prompt injection attack under the audit criteria.

content.value_statement: Trade show teams often lose leads because follow-up is late or too generic. This skill creates segmented email sequences that match lead quality, conversation context, and CRM workflow.
actual_capabilities: Creates hot, warm, and cold lead follow-up sequences after trade shows.
Writes thank-you emails, meeting recaps, and badge-scan outreach messages.
Suggests send timing for each lead tier after an event.
Adds bracketed personalization fields for names, companies, and conversation details.
Formats CRM-friendly merge tags for HubSpot, Salesforce, or generic workflows.
Provides subject line variants and practical follow-up tips.
limitations: Does not send emails or connect to a CRM by itself.
Requires user-provided lead details for strong personalization.
May include an author promotional footer unless removed by the user.
Does not verify legal compliance for email consent or regional marketing rules.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Fresh Trade Show Leads
use_cases.description: Turn badge scans and booth notes into hot, warm, and cold outreach plans within 48 hours.

use_cases.title: Create Event Follow-Up Campaigns
use_cases.description: Draft segmented nurture emails with timing, subject lines, and CRM merge fields.

use_cases.title: Send Personal Meeting Recaps
use_cases.description: Write concise recap emails that reference specific booth conversations and confirm next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Follow-Up Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with minimal event context
prompt_templates.prompt: MEDICA just ended. We sell sterilization monitoring systems. Write follow-up emails for leads we collected at the show.

prompt_templates.title: Segmented Badge Scan Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Lead volume is known but details are limited
prompt_templates.prompt: We collected 200 badge scans at Interpack. About 30 had real conversations, and 10 asked for pricing. Create follow-up sequences for each group.

prompt_templates.title: Personal Meeting Recap
prompt_templates.scenario: A specific contact needs a tailored message
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a meeting recap email for a prospect from CES. They asked about implementation timing, pricing, and whether our product works with Salesforce.

prompt_templates.title: CRM-Ready Nurture Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced campaign planning for a sales operations workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a post-show nurture plan for 350 leads from Hannover Messe. Use HubSpot merge tags, three lead tiers, two subject line variants per first email, and a recommended send schedule.

output_examples.input: We had 12 hot leads ask for quotes at MEDICA.
output_examples.output: A Day 1 email that references the booth conversation, confirms the requested quote, and proposes two meeting times. It also includes a Day 4 value-add follow-up and a Day 10 lower-commitment touch.

output_examples.input: We only scanned badges and do not know much about the contacts.
output_examples.output: A light cold-lead sequence that honestly says the team connected briefly, offers a useful resource, and avoids pretending there was a detailed conversation.

output_examples.input: Use Salesforce fields for first name and company.
output_examples.output: A CRM-ready email sequence with Salesforce-style name fields, bracketed personalization notes, and a send schedule by lead tier.

best_practices: Provide the show name, product, lead counts, and any notes from booth conversations.
Review every generated message for accuracy before adding it to an email tool.
Remove or replace promotional links if the final email must match your brand only.
anti_patterns: Do not send the same generic message to every scanned badge contact.
Do not claim a detailed conversation happened when the contact only visited briefly.
Do not use the sequence without checking consent, unsubscribe, and regional email rules.

faq.question: Can this skill send emails for me?
faq.answer: No. It drafts email content and timing plans, but it does not send messages or connect to email systems.

faq.question: Does it support CRM merge tags?
faq.answer: Yes. It can format examples for HubSpot, Salesforce, or generic bracketed placeholders.

faq.question: What lead information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the show name, product, lead counts, lead tiers, conversation notes, and desired next steps.

faq.question: Can it handle cold badge scans?
faq.answer: Yes. It creates lighter outreach that avoids overstating the relationship with the contact.

faq.question: Does it check email compliance?
faq.answer: No. You must verify consent, unsubscribe language, and applicable marketing laws before sending.

faq.question: Will it include external links?
faq.answer: The source skill includes Lensmor promotional links in documentation and output guidance. Review and remove them if needed.
